# Turmeric, Ginger, Garlic, Coriander
# Generated by KrishiMitra AI Knowledge Base Builder
# Source: ICAR guidelines + agricultural research literature
# Last updated: 2024-25 season

### ICAR Package of Practices for Spice Crops in India

---

#### **A. Turmeric (Curcuma longa)**

**Major States:** Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra  
**Varieties:** Pratibha, Suguna, Rajendra Sonia  

**Planting:**
- **Timing:** April-June
- **Method:** Plant in ridges and furrows

**Seed Rhizomes:**
- **Quantity:** 2500-3000 kg/ha

**Spacing:**
- **Row Spacing:** 45 cm
- **Plant Spacing:** 25 cm

**Fertilizer Application:**
- **N:P:K Ratio:** 120:60:120 kg/ha
- **Organic Manure:** 20-25 tonnes/ha

**Irrigation:**
- **Frequency:** Every 7-10 days
- **Number of Irrigations:** 15-20

**Pest Management:**
- **Shoot Borer (Conogethes punctiferalis):**
  - **Control:** Chlorpyrifos at 2.5 mL/L
- **Rhizome Rot (Pythium spp.):**
  - **Control:** Metalaxyl + Mancozeb
- **Leaf Blotch (Taphrina spp.):**
  - **Control:** Mancozeb at 2.5 g/L

**Disease Management:**
- **Avoid Waterlogging**

**Harvesting:**
- **Timing:** 7-9 months after planting
- **Method:** Boil rhizomes for 45-60 minutes, dry in the sun for 10-15 days

**Yield:**
- **Fresh Yield:** 200-300 q/ha
- **Dry Yield:** Reduce by a factor of 5:1 (i.e., 40-80 q/ha)

---

#### **B. Ginger (Zingiber officinale)**

**Major States:** Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra  
**Varieties:** Maran, Nadia, Suprabha  

**Planting:**
- **Timing:** March-May
- **Method:** Plant in ridges and furrows

**Seed Rhizomes:**
- **Quantity:** 1500-1800 kg/ha

**Spacing:**
- **Row Spacing:** 30 cm
- **Plant Spacing:** 20 cm

**Fertilizer Application:**
- **N:P:K Ratio:** 75:50:50 kg/ha + Organic Manure (25 t/ha)

**Shade Management:**
- **Ideal:** Soft shade of 60-70%

**Disease Management:**
- **Soft Rot (Pythium aphanidermatum):**
  - **Seed Treatment:** Metalaxyl at 6 g/kg
  - **Field Application:** Metalaxyl at 750 g/ha at planting

**Major Disease:**
- **Bacterial Wilt (Ralstonia spp.):**
  - **Control:** No specific treatment; destroy infected plants

**Yield:**
- **Fresh Yield:** 150-200 q/ha

---

#### **C. Garlic (Allium sativum)**

**Major States:** Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu  
**Varieties:** Yamuna Safed (G-50), G-1, Agrifound White  

**Sowing:**
- **Timing:** October-November in North India
- **Method:** Broadcast or line sowing

**Seed Splitting:**
- **Quantity:** 400-500 kg/ha

**Fertilizer Application:**
- **N:P:K Ratio:** 100:50:50 kg/ha
- **Basal Fertilization:** All P+K + 30 kg N
- **Top Dressing:** 70 kg N in two splits

**Irrigation:**
- **Frequency:** 8-10 times
- **Stop Irrigation:** 20 days before harvest

**Pest Management:**
- **Thrips Control:** Spinosad or Dimethoate

**Disease Management:**
- **Purple Blotch (Taphrina spp.):**
  - **Control:** Iprodione + Mancozeb at 1 mL/L

**Harvesting:**
- **Timing:** April-May
- **Method:** Dry for 2-3 weeks in shade

**Yield:**
- **Dry Yield:** 100-150 q/ha

---

#### **D. Coriander (Coriandrum sativum)**

**Major States:** Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu  
**Varieties:** Rajendra Swati, RCr-41, CS-6  

**Sowing:**
- **Timing:** October-November
- **Method:** Broadcast or line sowing (15-20 kg/ha)

**Fertilizer Application:**
- **N:P:K Ratio:** 30:30:20 kg/ha

**Irrigation:**
- **Frequency:** 3-4 times

**Pest Management:**
- **Stem Gall Control:** Remove and burn infected plants
- **Powdery Mildew Control:** Karathane at 1 mL/L

**Yield:**
- **Dry Seed Yield:** 8-12 q/ha

---

### Economic Threshold Levels (ETL) for Pests:
- **Shoot Borer (Conogethes punctiferalis):** ETL = 5 larvae per plant
- **Thrips:** ETL = 30% infested leaves
- **Purple Blotch (Taphrina spp.):** ETL = 10% affected plants

### Indicative Mandi Price Examples (not MSP):
- **Turmeric:** Rs. 7,000 per quintal
- **Ginger:** Rs. 3,000 per quintal
- **Garlic:** Rs. 1,800 per quintal
- **Coriander:** Rs. 4,000 per quintal

---

This structured guide provides detailed practices for the cultivation of turmeric, ginger, garlic, and coriander in India, ensuring optimal yields while managing pests and diseases effectively.
